title: OpenClaw MiniMax usage tracker should use GET for remains endpoint

source.license_note: Localized ACBench reproduction derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #63056.

task.summary: Repair the MiniMax usage request so the remains endpoint is called with the correct HTTP method.
task.instructions: Investigate why the usage tracker fails against the coding_plan/remains endpoint, repair the request method, and preserve the existing endpoint and auth header behavior.
task.acceptance_notes: Alternative fixes are allowed as long as the remains endpoint is called correctly without regressing the request shape.

visible_context.issue_text: The MiniMax usage tracker calls the remains endpoint with POST, but the endpoint only accepts GET and returns 404 for POST.
error_logs: status --usage shows MiniMax error after calling /coding_plan/remains
curl verification: GET works, POST returns 404
reproduction_steps: Run the unit tests from the repository root.
visible_context.notes: This fixture focuses on the request-shape bug around the MiniMax remains endpoint.

code_fault.description: The MiniMax remains endpoint is called with POST even though the API expects GET.

constraints.notes: This GitHub-derived reproduction is expected to be repaired in code, not by changing tests.
